args.command:
mkdir -p lib/wordcount test && cat > mix.exs <<'EOF'
defmodule Wordcount.MixProject do
  use Mix.Project

  def project do
    [
      app: :wordcount,
      version: "0.1.0",
      elixir: "~> 1.14",
      start_permanent: Mix.env() == :prod,
      escript: [main_module: Wordcount.CLI],
      deps: deps()
    ]
  end

  def application do
    [
      extra_applications: [:logger]
    ]
  end

  defp deps do
    []
  end
end
EOF
cat > lib/wordcount.ex <<'EOF'
defmodule Wordcount do
  @moduledoc """
  Provides word-counting helpers for text and files.
  """

  @word_regex ~r/[\p{L}\p{N}_]+(?:['’-][\p{L}\p{N}_]+)*/u

  @doc """
  Counts words in a string.

  Words are contiguous runs of letters, numbers, or underscores, optionally
  joined by apostrophes or hyphens. Punctuation and whitespace are ignored.
  """
  @spec count_words(String.t()) :: non_neg_integer()
  def count_words(text) when is_binary(text) do
    text
    |> Regex.scan(@word_regex)
    |> length()
  end
end
EOF
cat > lib/wordcount/cli.ex <<'EOF'
defmodule Wordcount.CLI do
  @moduledoc """
  Command-line interface for counting words in a file.
  """

  @doc """
  Entry point for `mix run` and the generated escript.
  """
  @spec main([String.t()]) :: term()
  def main(args \\ System.argv()) do
    case args do
      [path] ->
        path
        |> File.read()
        |> case do
          {:ok, contents} ->
            IO.puts(Wordcount.count_words(contents))

          {:error, reason} ->
            IO.puts(:stderr, "error: could not read #{path}: #{:file.format_error(reason)}")
            System.halt(1)
        end

      [] ->
        usage(:stderr)
        System.halt(2)

      ["--help" | _] ->
        usage(:stdout)
        System.halt(0)

      _ ->
        usage(:stderr)
        System.halt(2)
    end
  end

  defp usage(io_device) do
    IO.puts(io_device, "Usage: wordcount FILE")
  end
end
EOF

* creating README.md

* creating test/wordcount_cli_test.exs

Your Mix project was created successfully.
You can use "mix" to compile it, test it, and more:

    cd wordcount_cli
    mix test

Run "mix help" for more commands.

defmodule WordcountCli do
  @moduledoc """
  Documentation for `WordcountCli`.
  """

  @doc """
  Hello world.

  ## Examples

      iex> WordcountCli.hello()
      :world

  """
  def hello do
    :world
  end
end

defmodule WordcountCli.MixProject do
  use Mix.Project

  def project do
    [
      app: :wordcount_cli,
      version: "0.1.0",
      elixir: "~> 1.14",
      start_permanent: Mix.env() == :prod,
      deps: deps()
    ]
  end

  # Run "mix help compile.app" to learn about applications.
  def application do
    [
      extra_applications: [:logger],
      mod: {WordcountCli.Application, []}
    ]
  end

  # Run "mix help deps" to learn about dependencies.
  defp deps do
    [
      # {:dep_from_hexpm, "~> 0.3.0"},
      # {:dep_from_git, git: "https://github.com/elixir-lang/my_dep.git", tag: "0.1.0"}
    ]
  end
end

defmodule WordcountCliTest do
  use ExUnit.Case
  doctest WordcountCli

  test "greets the world" do
    assert WordcountCli.hello() == :world
  end
end

defmodule WordcountCli.Application do
  # See https://hexdocs.pm/elixir/Application.html
  # for more information on OTP Applications
  @moduledoc false

  use Application

  @impl true
  def start(_type, _args) do
    children = [
      # Starts a worker by calling: WordcountCli.Worker.start_link(arg)
      # {WordcountCli.Worker, arg}
    ]

    # See https://hexdocs.pm/elixir/Supervisor.html
    # for other strategies and supported options
    opts = [strategy: :one_for_one, name: WordcountCli.Supervisor]
    Supervisor.start_link(children, opts)
  end
end

#!/bin/sh

script_dir=$(CDPATH= cd -- "$(dirname -- "$0")" && pwd)
status_file="$script_dir/status.txt"

state=""
port=""

while IFS='=' read -r key value; do
    case "$key" in
        state) state=$value ;;
        port) port=$value ;;
    esac
done < "$status_file"

if [ "$state" = "ok" ] && [ "$port" = "8080" ]; then
    echo "HEALTHY state=ok port=8080"
    exit 0
fi

echo "UNHEALTHY state=$state port=$port"
exit 1
